title: Support soft constraints in randomization
body:
## Summary

Add support for SystemVerilog soft constraints (IEEE 1800-2017 18.5.13). Soft constraints are optional - they are satisfied when possible, but dropped when they conflict with hard constraints.

### Changes

- Add `RANDOMIZER_SOFT` to `VCMethod` enum in `V3AstAttr.h`
- Modify `V3Randomize.cpp` to use `RANDOMIZER_SOFT` when `nodep->isSoft()` is true
- Add `soft()` method and `m_softConstraints` vector to `VlRandomizer` class
- Modify `VlRandomizer::next()` to implement two-pass constraint solving:
  1. First try solving with hard + soft constraints
  2. If UNSAT, retry with only hard constraints

### Example

```systemverilog
class example;
   rand bit [7:0] value;
   
   // Hard constraint: must satisfy
   constraint c_hard { value > 100; }
   
   // Soft constraint: prefer this, but drop if conflicts
   constraint c_soft { soft value < 50; }
endclass
```

In this example, the soft constraint conflicts with the hard constraint, so it will be dropped and `randomize()` will succeed with values > 100.

## Test plan

- [x] Created `t_randomize_soft.v` test case covering:
  - Basic soft constraints that can be satisfied
  - Soft constraints that conflict with hard constraints (soft should be dropped)
  - Multiple soft constraints working together
- [x] Lint test passes
